req.title: Card freeze / unfreeze
req.statement: A customer shall freeze or unfreeze their card instantly; frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three primary concern areas: (1) valid freeze/unfreeze toggling for a card owned by the caller, (2) rejection of authorization attempts on a frozen card, and (3) error handling for non-existent cards. I designed five tests to cover the full state machine (active→frozen→active), the authorization rejection invariant, the 404 error path, a rapid idempotent double-freeze, and a boundary case where the card ID is at the minimum valid identifier length. Heuristics applied: EP for the happy-path toggle, STC for the full freeze→unfreeze cycle, NEG for the missing card and frozen-card authorization rejection, BVA for the minimal card ID boundary, and DT for the freeze/authorization decision table.

tests.name: test_freeze_active_card_returns_200_and_frozen_state
preconditions: Caller is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Card identified by CARD_ID exists and belongs to the caller
Card is currently in ACTIVE state
actions: Inspect HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains the correct card ID
trace: R-FIN-010: customer shall freeze card instantly
tests.executable:
def test_freeze_active_card_returns_200_and_frozen_state(api_client, active_card):
    card_id = active_card["id"]
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze",
        json={"action": "freeze"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "FROZEN"
    assert body["id"] == card_id
    # teardown: restore state
    api_client.post(f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze", json={"action": "unfreeze"})

tests.name: test_freeze_then_unfreeze_card_full_state_cycle
preconditions: Caller is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Card identified by CARD_ID exists and belongs to the caller
Card is currently in ACTIVE state
trace: R-FIN-010: customer shall freeze or unfreeze card instantly
tests.executable:
def test_freeze_then_unfreeze_card_full_state_cycle(api_client, active_card):
    card_id = active_card["id"]
    freeze_resp = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze",
        json={"action": "freeze"},
    )
    assert freeze_resp.status_code == 200
    assert freeze_resp.json()["status"] == "FROZEN"

    unfreeze_resp = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze",
        json={"action": "unfreeze"},
    )
    assert unfreeze_resp.status_code == 200
    assert unfreeze_resp.json()["status"] == "ACTIVE"

tests.name: test_authorization_rejected_when_card_is_frozen
preconditions: Caller is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Card identified by CARD_ID exists, belongs to caller, and is FROZEN
actions: Freeze the card via POST /cards/{CARD_ID}/freeze with action=freeze
Attempt an authorization via POST /authorizations with the frozen card ID
Inspect HTTP response status and decline reason
expected: Response body contains decline_reason=CARD_FROZEN or equivalent
trace: R-FIN-010: frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts
tests.executable:
def test_authorization_rejected_when_card_is_frozen(api_client, active_card):
    card_id = active_card["id"]
    api_client.post(f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze", json={"action": "freeze"})

    auth_resp = api_client.post(
        "/authorizations",
        json={"card_id": card_id, "amount": 100, "currency": "USD"},
    )
    assert auth_resp.status_code in (402, 403)
    body = auth_resp.json()
    assert body.get("decline_reason") == "CARD_FROZEN"

    # teardown
    api_client.post(f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze", json={"action": "unfreeze"})

tests.name: test_freeze_nonexistent_card_returns_404
preconditions: Caller is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Card ID used does not exist in the system
actions: Inspect HTTP response status and error body
expected: Response body contains error code indicating card not found
trace: R-FIN-010: 404 when card does not exist
tests.executable:
import uuid

def test_freeze_nonexistent_card_returns_404(api_client):
    nonexistent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{nonexistent_id}/freeze",
        json={"action": "freeze"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 404
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body

tests.name: test_freeze_card_with_minimum_length_id
preconditions: Caller is authenticated with a valid bearer token
A card exists whose ID is exactly at the minimum permissible length (1 character or system minimum)
actions: Retrieve or create a card with a minimum-length ID via <fixture: min_id_card>
Inspect HTTP response status and returned state
expected: No truncation or routing errors due to short ID
trace: BVA on card identifier length boundary
tests.executable:
def test_freeze_card_with_minimum_length_id(api_client, min_id_card):
    card_id = min_id_card["id"]
    assert len(str(card_id)) >= 1, "Card ID must meet minimum length"

    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze",
        json={"action": "freeze"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["status"] == "FROZEN"

    # teardown: restore to ACTIVE
    api_client.post(f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze", json={"action": "unfreeze"})
